text:
Admission Date:  2009-10-22              Discharge Date:   2000-09-29

Date of Birth:  1985-03-08             Sex:   M

Service: CARDIOTHORACIC

Allergies:
Atorvastatin

Attending:Beverly
Chief Complaint:
Shortness of breath

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
2009-10-22 Aortic valve replacement with a 23-mm Steele Magna
bioprosthesis

History of Present Illness:
Mr. Schmidt is an 80 year old gentleman with severe aortic
stenosis who recently has developed increased shortness of
breath.  He was referred to Alliancehealth Clinton
for surgical evaluation.

Past Medical History:
Aortic Stenosis, Hypertension, Fisher Disease, Hypertension,
Hypercholesterolemia, carotid stenosis, Gastroesophageal reflux
disease, Spinal stenosis with chronic right leg pain, Scoliosis,
s/p laminectomy x2, s/p Rt Knee repair x3, s/p Rt Shoulder
surgery x2, left shoulder x1

Social History:
He has been married for 28 years. He spends 25-mid 03 in
[**State 108**] where he is now a resident. He continues to smoke a pipe
up to 5 times daily and drinks 2-3 glasses of wine/wk

Family History:
Parents are deceased (father 48 years, cancer; mother - [**Age over 90 **]
years, cancer). A daughter has multiple sclerosis.

Physical Exam:
Pulse: 91 Resp: 22 O2 sat: 98% RA Temp 99.8
B/P  Right: 108/56    Left:
Height: 66 inch    Weight: 65.7 kg

General: no acute distress

bilateral arms, right < left calf with venous statis
discoloration
HEENT: PERRLA [x] EOMI [x]
Neck:  Supple [x] Full ROM [x] no lymphadenopathy

Heart: RRR []  Irregular [x]  Murmur 3/6 systolic
Abdomen: Soft [x] non-distended [x] non-tender [x]  bowel sounds
+ [x] no palpable masses
Extremities: Warm [x], well-perfused [x] Edema + 2 pitting pedal

Varicosities: superfical bilateral
Neuro: Grossly intact
Pulses:
Femoral      Right: +2  Left: +2
DP           Right: +2  Left: +2
PT           Austin Young: +2  Left: +2
Radial       Right: +2  Left: +2

Carotid Bruit        Right: murmur    Left: no bruit

Pertinent Results:
2009-10-22 Echo: PRE-BYPASS: The left atrium is mildly dilated. No
spontaneous echo contrast or thrombus is seen in the body of the
left atrium or left atrial appendage. No atrial septal defect is
seen by 2D or color Doppler. There is mild symmetric left
ventricular hypertrophy. The left ventricular cavity size is
normal. Overall left ventricular systolic function is normal
(LVEF>55%). Right ventricular chamber size and free wall motion
are normal. There are simple atheroma in the ascending aorta.
There are complex (>4mm) atheroma in the descending thoracic
aorta. There are three aortic valve leaflets. The aortic valve
leaflets are severely thickened/deformed. There is critical
aortic valve stenosis (valve area <0.8cm2). Mild (1+) aortic
regurgitation is seen. The aortic regurgitation jet is
eccentric. The mitral valve leaflets are moderately thickened.
There is moderate thickening of the mitral valve chordae. There
is no pericardial effusion. Epi-aortic scan performed to
identify areas in ascending aorta free of atheroma.
POST: 1. Preserved Bhc Fairfax Hospital North-ventricular systolic function 2.
Bioprosthetic valve in aortic position.Well seated and
mechamically stable. Good leaflet excursion. Trace valvular AI.
Peal gradient 22 mm Hg, Mean gradient 14 mm Hg. 3. No other
change

Labs:
2000-09-29 WBC-7.1
2010-02-21 WBC-12.2* RBC-3.56* Hgb-11.4* Hct-33.7* Plt Ct-137*
2009-10-22 WBC-11.7* RBC-2.20*# Hgb-7.4*# Hct-22.1*# Plt Ct-100*

HCO3-23

TotBili-1.1
2010-02-21 Albumin-3.8 Mg-2.7*

CXR: 2010-02-21 FINDINGS: In comparison with study of 07-18, there
is some decrease in the bibasilar opacification, though some
atelectasis and effusion persist.  Prosthetic valve and midline
sternal sutures are unchanged. No definite vascular congestion.



Brief Hospital Course:
Mr. Schmidt was a same day admit after undergoing pre-operative
work-up as an outpatient. On day of admission he was brought
directly to the operating room and underwent an aortic valve
replacement. Please see operative report for surgical details.
Following surgery he was transferred to the CVICU for invasive
monitoring in stable condition. Within 24 hours he was weaned
from sedation, awoke neurologically intact and extubated. On
post-op day two his chest tubes were removed and he was
transferred to the telemetry floor for further care. He
developed episodes of atrial fibrillation which was treated with
beta blockers and amiodarone.  He converted into a regular sinus
rhythm within twelve hours on that regimen.  His epicardial
wires were removed and he was diuresed toward his pre-operative
weight.  His renal functioned was elevated to a peak Cre 2.6.
He was seen by renal who felt this was contrast induced.  His
lasix was held.  He autodiuresed.  On discharge his Cre was 1.4.
 He was restarted on PO lasix on discharge and will have his
renal functioned rechecked in 1 week.  He made steady progress
and was discharge to home on post-operative day four.  His
follow-up appointment were advised.

by mouth once a day

Discharge Disposition:

PMH: Hypertension, Fisher Disease, Hypertension,
Hypercholesterolemia, carotid stenosis, Gastroesophageal reflux

s/p laminectomy x2, s/p Rt Knee repair x3, s/p Rt Shoulder

Discharge Condition:
good

Discharge Instructions:
Please shower daily including washing incisions, no baths or
swimming
Monitor wounds for infection - redness, drainage, or increased
pain
Report any fever greater than 101
Report any weight gain of greater than 2 pounds in 24 hours or 5
pounds in a week
No creams, lotions, powders, or ointments to incisions
No driving for approximately one month
No lifting more than 10 pounds for 10 weeks
Please call with any questions or concerns 489-777-3285

Followup Instructions:
Dr. Wilson (cardiac surgery) in 4 weeks (518-695-7030.
Dr. Collins (cardiology) in 04-04 weeks.
Dr. Ferguson (615-356-8387 in 09-24 weeks.
